results.name: prod secure context + SW controls (non-localhost ⇒ cache-first mode)
PROD cache-first confirmed — the boot path runs the verify+cache+OPFS branch, NOT the DEV dev-fresh path
results.detail: first fetch x-holo-cache:miss (miss = cold prod fetch, cached + OPFS-written; dev-fresh would mean the integration path was skipped)

results.name: the SW WRITES VERIFIED bytes through to OPFS — κ present in the SW's holo-fhs-kstore
DURABILITY — after Cache Storage is cleared, the SW still serves from OPFS (x-holo-cache: opfs), byte-identical (L3 survives Cache-Storage eviction)

spec: The OpfsKappaStore (-04-) is wired into holo-fhs-sw.js as a durable tier behind KCACHE. Witnessed in real Chromium in PROD cache-first mode (forced locally via an HTTPS proxy on a non-localhost host so the SW's DEV bypass is off): the SW writes verified bytes through to OPFS, and after Cache Storage is cleared it still serves them network-free from OPFS (x-holo-cache: opfs), byte-identical — durable storage surviving Cache-Storage eviction (Law L3). On localhost the integration is a guarded no-op (every closure entry is a dev-fresh source file); the no-regression property (SW installs+controls with the new import) is shown by holo-sw-opfs-persist-witness.mjs.
